# CWE-76 positive: neutralizes < but not URL-encoded equivalents (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-76-vulnerable.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"net/http"
	"strings"
)

// CWE-76 Improper Neutralization of Equivalent Special Elements
// Vulnerable line: strips literal angle brackets only before HTML output.
func WritePreview(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
		http.Error(w, "method", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
		return
	}
	if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "form", http.StatusBadRequest)
		return
	}
	raw := r.FormValue("snippet")
	if raw == "" {
		http.Error(w, "empty", http.StatusBadRequest)
		return
	}
	safe := strings.ReplaceAll(raw, "<", "")
	safe = strings.ReplaceAll(safe, ">", "")
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
	_, _ = w.Write([]byte("<article>" + safe + "</article>"))
}
